Stop being scared of losing money lol. That fear is why most people never even start. We watch others win while we sit back worried about getting scammed. But risk comes before reward, no pain no gain. Ask questions, learn from people doing it, don't let fear stop you. I used to be scared too but once I pushed past it and stayed focused, everything changed!

I’ll be honest—this journey completely changed my life. I went from having almost nothing to building a home in Florida, starting with just a $3,000 account and growing it to $41,600 in only two weeks. That breakthrough taught me something powerful: success in the market isn’t about chasing perfect timing, it’s about staying consistent and committed—time in the market beats timing the market.

Trading advice for anyone new to crypto: try not to focus too much on the 1-minute charts. When I first started trading, I made the mistake of reacting to every small price movement, and it cost me a lot of money. Constantly watching the smallest timeframes can create unnecessary stress and lead to impulsive decisions.

Becoming a good trader takes time and patience. When i first got into trading i was liquidated twice, and lost my entire mortgage deposit. I could have given up, but decided to learn how to trade and put it into practice. 2 years later and I'm up with 842k.

Unfortunately, not all of us were financially literate early. I was 35 when I finally educated myself and started taking steps. I went from $176,000 in debt with zero savings or retirement to now, 2 years later, fully debt-free and over $1000,000 net worth. I know that doesn't SOUND like a lot, but I'm incredibly proud of it. Now I'm fast-tracking my wealth building (investing $400,000 annually) and don't owe a dime to anyone. It's a good feeling!

I’ve seen so many “$50 to $5K” videos that it’s hard not to roll my eyes anymore. Either they’re using insane leverage, demo accounts, or pure luck. I’ve blown small accounts trying to flip them fast  every time ends the same.
But if this guy actually did it with structure and discipline, not gambling… I’m all ears. Because turning small capital into consistency is the real skill I’m chasing.

As a newbie about to invest, you must have these four things in mind 
1. Have a long term mindset.
2. Be willing to take risk.
3. Be careful on money usage, if you're not spending to earn back, then stop spending.
4. Never claim to know - Ask questions and it's best you work with a financial advisor like Evan Dennis.

Regrettably, many of us lacked financial literacy in our early years. I was 35 when I began to educate myself and take meaningful action. I transformed my situation from being $176,000 in debt with no savings or retirement plan to being completely debt-free and having a net worth of over $1,000,000 just two years later. While it might not seem like a huge amount to some, I take great pride in this achievement. Now, I'm accelerating my wealth-building journey by investing $400,000 each year, and I'm debt-free. It's an empowering feeling!

I’d like to point out that your first back test @6:04 . The entire range was a Fair Value Gap in side the Open High and Open low, along with displacement. This is one of the ways I trade the ORB and it’s super profitable with a 2:1 risk to reward. We’re not gonna see the pull back all the time, however you will see displacement and FVG’s. Alright I looked over some small cap stocks called out in my program’s discord chat and backtested this strategy against those. A few had good set ups to the downside that would have worked here but can’t buy puts on these so I looked for a good example where it crossed resistance with strong candle and retested and this strategy would have worked perfectly . As long as there’s patience for the correct setup, I feel like the success rate will be promising . If not, that’s why stop losses exist. Excited to test this out next week and report back. Thank you!
